name: Helsinn Healthcare S.A. v. Teva Pharmaceuticals USA Inc.
question: Does an inventor’s sale of an invention to a third party who is obligated to keep the invention confidential qualify as prior art for purposes of determining the patentability of the invention?
description: A case in which the Court held that, under the Leahy-Smith America Invents Act, an inventor’s sale of an invention to a third party invalidates the patent, even if the third party is obligated to keep the invention confidential.
